title: CLI: Output generated extension method source code for service types
description:
Enhance the IoCTools CLI to output the generated source code for service collection extension methods.

**Current State:**
- `services-path` outputs the path to the generated registration extension file
- `services` outputs a summary of registrations (lifetimes, interface/implementation pairings)
- Users must navigate to the file to see actual generated code

**Requested Feature:**
Add a command (or flag) to output the actual generated source code for:
1. The full extension method file (all registrations)
2. Registration code for a specific service type (filtered view)

**Use Cases:**
- Quickly inspect what IoCTools generates without finding/opening files
- Copy-paste generated code for documentation or debugging
- CI/scripting integration where raw source output is needed
- Understanding how a specific service gets registered

**Proposed Implementation:**
- New `services-source` command or `--source` flag on `services`
- Optional `--type` filter to show only specific service registrations
- Output to stdout by default for piping/redirection
close_reason: recreating with new prefix

title: Stabilize IoCTools analyzers for Delta compatibility
description:
Stabilize IoCTools analyzer rules for Delta project compatibility. Key goals:

**Implicit Lifetime Behavior:**
- No IOC069/070/071 when lifetime is implicit default
- Explicit `[Scoped]` redundant with implicit default → IOC033
- Configurable default implicit lifetime (likely scoped)

**Hosted Services:**
- Pure hosted service → implicit lifetime only; explicit is redundant (IOC072)
- Hosted service + extra interfaces must specify lifetime (IOC070)

**Cross-Assembly Skip:**
- Skip IOC001/IOC002 when dependency implementation lives in another assembly
- Clean architecture: Application shouldn't reference Infrastructure

**Framework Dependencies:**
- ILogger, IConfiguration, options wrappers must NOT trigger IOC001

**Severity Expectations:**
- Missing dep, circular dep → errors
- Manual registration clash → error-level

Outstanding work items documented in prompt.md including Delta build fixes.
close_reason: recreating with new prefix

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
**Outstanding Work Items (from prompt.md):**

1. **Delta build error**: Fix HttpRequestRepository expecting DbSet<HttpRequestRecord> missing in DeltaDbContext
2. **Cross-assembly IOC001 noise**: Reintroduce/ensure skip when dependency assembly differs from consumer
3. **Severity changes**: Ship IoCTools with missing dep, circular dep, manual-registration clash as errors by default
4. **Manual reg analyzer**: Add detection of manual service registration when IoCTools already registers (severity = error)
5. **Hosted service redundancy**: Ensure IOC072 covers explicit lifetime on pure hosted; hosted+interfaces needs lifetime (IOC070)
6. **Mediator skip / framework dep skip**: Confirm IOC001 not raised for Mediator handlers or framework abstractions
7. **Investigate remaining ~6 warnings in Delta**: Verify each is valid; no false positives

**Files touched (IoCTools):**
- DiagnosticRules.cs - cross-assembly logic
- RedundantConfigurationValidator.cs - implicit/hosted lifetime suppression
- DiagnosticDescriptors.cs - IOC072 wording
- TypeSkipEvaluator.cs + GeneratorStyleOptions.cs - Mediator handler skips
- Tests: HostedServiceLifetimeTests.cs, MissingLifetimeSuggestionTests.cs, FrameworkDependencySkipTests.cs
- README.md - implicit lifetime behavior

Note: Much of this work appears to be in the uncommitted changes. Needs verification of completion status.

title: BUG: Fix 16 pre-existing test failures with incorrect severity expectations
description:
16 tests fail because they expect the wrong diagnostic severity. Examples:
- IOC013 expected Error but is Warning (per DiagnosticDescriptors.cs)
- IOC006 expected Error but is Warning
- IOC008 expected Error but is Warning
- IOC040 expected Error but is Warning
- IOC019 expected Error but is Warning
- MSBuild configuration tests expect Error but get configured values (Info/Hidden/Warning)

These tests were written before severities were finalized and have incorrect expectations.

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-5f1

CONTEXT: While working on adding severity assertions to tests, discovered 16 pre-existing test failures. These tests have severity assertions but the expectations are incorrect - they expect a different severity than what DiagnosticDescriptors.cs defines.

Examples of incorrect expectations:
- IOC013 (Singleton->Transient) tests expect Error but DiagnosticDescriptors says Warning
- IOC006 (Duplicate dependency) tests expect Error but DiagnosticDescriptors says Warning  
- IOC008 (Duplicate in same attribute) tests expect Error but DiagnosticDescriptors says Warning
- IOC040 (Redundant declarations) tests expect Error but DiagnosticDescriptors says Warning
- IOC019 (Static field) tests expect Error but DiagnosticDescriptors says Warning

MSBuild configuration tests are a special case - they configure diagnostic severity dynamically and should not have hardcoded severity assertions.

FILES AFFECTED:
- LifetimeDependencyAdvancedGenericTests.cs
- LifetimeDependencyIntegrationTests.cs
- RedundancyDetectionTests.cs
- ConfigurationInjectionDiagnosticsTests.cs
- MSBuildDiagnosticConfigurationTests.cs

title: Fix diagnostic ID collisions in DiagnosticDescriptors
description:
Several diagnostic IDs are used for multiple different diagnostics:

**Collisions found:**
- IOC048: NullableDependencyNotAllowed AND InheritedLifetimeRedundant
- IOC050: ManualRegistrationDuplicatesIoCTools AND DependencySetCycleDetected
- IOC051: ManualRegistrationLifetimeMismatch AND DependencySetNameCollision
- IOC054: DependencySetNearMatchSuggestion AND RedundantMemberName
- IOC073: ManualOptionsRegistrationDuplicatesIoCTools AND ManualRegistrationCouldUseAttributes

This will cause confusing diagnostic output and breaks the ID → diagnostic mapping for tools.

**Fix:** Assign unique IDs to each diagnostic. May need to reserve a higher range (IOC081+) for new diagnostics.
close_reason: Duplicate of ioc-6zq - already fixed in commit 935a754. IDs reassigned to IOC081-IOC086.

title: BUG: Duplicate diagnostic IDs in DiagnosticDescriptors.cs

- IOC048: InheritedLifetimeRedundant vs NullableDependencyNotAllowed

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

title: Stabilize IoCTools analyzers for Delta compatibility
description:
Stabilize IoCTools analyzer rules for Delta project compatibility. Key goals:

**Implicit Lifetime Behavior:**
- No IOC069/070/071 when lifetime is implicit default
- Explicit `[Scoped]` redundant with implicit default → IOC033
- Configurable default implicit lifetime (likely scoped)

**Hosted Services:**
- Pure hosted service → implicit lifetime only; explicit is redundant (IOC072)
- Hosted service + extra interfaces must specify lifetime (IOC070)

**Cross-Assembly Skip:**
- Skip IOC001/IOC002 when dependency implementation lives in another assembly
- Clean architecture: Application shouldn't reference Infrastructure

**Framework Dependencies:**
- ILogger, IConfiguration, options wrappers must NOT trigger IOC001

**Severity Expectations:**
- Missing dep, circular dep → errors
- Manual registration clash → error-level

Outstanding work items documented in prompt.md including Delta build fixes.
close_reason: recreating with new prefix

2. **Cross-assembly IOC001 noise**: Reintroduce/ensure skip when dependency assembly differs from consumer
3. **Severity changes**: Ship IoCTools with missing dep, circular dep, manual-registration clash as errors by default
4. **Manual reg analyzer**: Add detection of manual service registration when IoCTools already registers (severity = error)
5. **Hosted service redundancy**: Ensure IOC072 covers explicit lifetime on pure hosted; hosted+interfaces needs lifetime (IOC070)
6. **Mediator skip / framework dep skip**: Confirm IOC001 not raised for Mediator handlers or framework abstractions
7. **Investigate remaining ~6 warnings in Delta**: Verify each is valid; no false positives

**Files touched (IoCTools):**
- DiagnosticRules.cs - cross-assembly logic

- README.md - implicit lifetime behavior

Note: Much of this work appears to be in the uncommitted changes. Needs verification of completion status.

title: Manual Integration Testing Epic: Delta Repository
description:
Comprehensive manual testing of IoCTools against the Delta repository (ComputerCraft/Lua Minecraft automation kernel) which heavily uses this project.

**Goals:**
- Exercise all IoCTools CLI scenarios and parameter combinations against a real-world consumer
- Validate diagnostics fire correctly in complex inheritance/DI scenarios
- Identify bugs, UX issues, and documentation gaps through hands-on testing

**Testing Approach:**
- Systematic exploration of all lifetime attributes ([Scoped], [Singleton], [Transient])
- All dependency injection patterns ([Inject], [DependsOn], [InjectConfiguration])
- All registration patterns ([RegisterAs], [RegisterAsAll], InstanceSharing modes)
- Cross-assembly scenarios
- Inheritance hierarchies
- Background services (IHostedService)
- Conditional services
- Diagnostic validation (all IOC001-IOC086 where applicable)

**Issue Handling Protocol:**
- **BUGS**: Create task with "reproduce first with test, then fix" approach
- **UX ISSUES**: Create sp:brainstorming task to discuss before implementing
- **DOC GAPS**: Create docs task with specific location and improvement needed

**Success Criteria:**
- All major feature combinations tested against Delta
- All discovered issues tracked with appropriate bead type
- No critical bugs remaining unaddressed

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
ISSUE HANDLING PROTOCOL:

When discovering issues during testing:

**BUGS (something broken):**
1. Create task: 'BUG: [description]'
2. Include reproduction steps in description
3. AC must include: 'Add failing test that reproduces the bug' THEN 'Fix the bug'
4. Parent: this epic (ioc-7qi)

**UX ISSUES (confusing, poor ergonomics):**
1. Create task with sp:brainstorming label: 'UX: [description]'
2. Document current behavior and why it's problematic
3. Suggest 2-3 options if possible
4. Parent: this epic (ioc-7qi)

**DOC GAPS (missing/unclear documentation):**
1. Create task: 'DOCS: [description]'
2. Specify which file and what's missing/wrong
3. Parent: this epic (ioc-7qi)

title: Test lifetime attributes against Delta
description:
Test all lifetime attribute combinations in Delta:
- [Scoped] services
- [Singleton] services  
- [Transient] services
- Inherited lifetimes from base classes
- Lifetime conflicts (Singleton depending on Scoped)
- Mixed lifetimes in inheritance hierarchies

Verify diagnostics fire correctly for violations (IOC012-IOC015).

title: Test dependency injection patterns against Delta
description:
Test all DI patterns in Delta:
- [Inject] field injection (private, readonly variations)
- [DependsOn<T>] single and multiple type parameters
- [DependsOn] with MemberNames for custom naming
- Mixed [Inject] + [DependsOn] on same class
- Inheritance with injected fields in base classes
- Collection injection (IEnumerable<T>, IReadOnlyCollection<T>)

Verify constructor generation and diagnostics (IOC043-IOC045, IOC070).

title: Test configuration injection against Delta
description:
Test configuration injection in Delta:
- [InjectConfiguration] with section paths
- [InjectConfiguration] with primitive types (string, int, bool)
- [InjectConfiguration] with complex objects
- SupportsReloading = true scenarios
- DefaultValue and Required options
- Mixed configuration + service injection

Verify options binding generation and diagnostics (IOC057, IOC079, IOC083).

title: Test registration patterns against Delta
description:
Test all registration patterns in Delta:
- [RegisterAs<T>] single interface
- [RegisterAs<T1, T2, ...>] multiple interfaces (up to 8)
- [RegisterAsAll] for all implemented interfaces
- InstanceSharing.Separate vs InstanceSharing.Shared
- RegisterAs-only services (no direct class registration)
- Inheritance with RegisterAs on base classes

Verify registration generation and diagnostics (IOC029-IOC031, IOC063-IOC065, IOC069).

title: Test background services against Delta
description:
Test hosted service scenarios in Delta:
- IHostedService implementations
- BackgroundService subclasses
- Hosted services with [Inject] dependencies
- Hosted services with configuration injection
- Multiple hosted services
- Hosted service lifetime validation

Verify automatic registration and diagnostics (IOC011, IOC014, IOC072).

title: Test cross-assembly scenarios against Delta
description:
Test cross-assembly DI in Delta:
- Services depending on types from other assemblies
- [ExternalService] marking for external dependencies
- Framework dependencies (ILogger, IConfiguration, IHttpClientFactory)
- Cross-project diagnostic validation
- Assembly scanning boundaries

Verify cross-assembly awareness and diagnostics (IOC001-IOC002).

title: Test inheritance hierarchies against Delta
description:
Test complex inheritance in Delta:
- Multi-level inheritance (3+ levels)
- Abstract base classes with [Inject] fields
- Virtual/override patterns with DI
- Diamond inheritance scenarios (if applicable)
- Base class lifetime inheritance
- Constructor chaining with dependencies

Verify constructor generation handles all inheritance scenarios.

title: Test diagnostic coverage against Delta
description:
Systematically verify diagnostic coverage in Delta:
- Trigger each relevant diagnostic (IOC001-IOC086)
- Verify error messages are clear and actionable
- Check diagnostic severity is appropriate
- Validate fix suggestions work
- Document any diagnostics that don't fire when expected
- Document any false positives

Focus on real-world scenarios that Delta exercises.

title: Review CLI UX and error messages against Delta
description:
Review overall developer experience when using IoCTools with Delta:
- Are error messages clear and actionable?
- Is the fix guidance helpful?
- Are there confusing diagnostic names or descriptions?
- Do diagnostics point to the right location?
- Is the generated code readable/debuggable?
- Are there missing warnings for common mistakes?

Document UX issues as sp:brainstorming tasks for discussion.

title: BUG: Investigate LifetimeDependencyIntegrationTests timeout flakiness
close_reason: Unable to reproduce: test passes consistently (~1s vs 20s threshold). Closed as not a current issue.

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: session-reflect

TEST: LifetimeDependencyIntegrationTests.LifetimeIntegration_LargeServiceHierarchyWithLifetimeValidation_CompletesInReasonableTime

BEHAVIOR: Test times out or is skipped during normal test runs

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Identify root cause (performance bottleneck vs infinite recursion)
- [ ] Fix or adjust timeout as appropriate
- [ ] Test passes consistently in CI

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
INVESTIGATION RESULTS:

Cannot reproduce the timeout/flakiness issue.

TEST RESULTS:
- 5 consecutive runs: ALL PASSED (~1 second each)
- Full test suite: 1223 passed, 1 skipped (different test), 0 failed
- Performance: Test completes in ~1s vs 20s timeout threshold

TIMEOUT THRESHOLDS (generous):
- LargeServiceHierarchy: 20 seconds (completes in ~1s)
- ComplexDependencyTree: 15 seconds
- ManyLifetimeViolations: 10 seconds

POSSIBLE EXPLANATIONS FOR ORIGINAL REPORT:
1. Transient environmental issue at time of discovery
2. Fixed by subsequent changes (no changes to test file found)
3. CI environment-specific (but CI doesn't run tests)

RECOMMENDATION: Close as unable to reproduce. If issue recurs, reopen with specific reproduction steps.

title: TASK: Restore and modernize generator performance benchmarks
description:
GeneratorPerformanceTests.cs and PipelineBenchmarks.cs were removed during v1.3.0 refactor. Current replacement is a basic scalability check (20 services in <5s) which is a sanity check, not a regression detector. Re-implement robust benchmarking to track generation speed/memory usage over time.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Benchmarking tests that measure generator performance
- [ ] Can detect performance regressions vs baseline
- [ ] Runs in CI or can be run manually
- [ ] Documents baseline performance numbers

comments.author: Nathan Whittle

title: CLEANUP: Rename ambiguous AppSettings class in sample project
description:
AppSettings exists in both Configuration/ and Services/ namespaces in the sample project. This can confuse users studying the sample and exposed a generator bug (now fixed) with type name ambiguity.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Rename one AppSettings to be more specific (e.g., ServiceAppSettings or rename by feature area)
- [ ] Update any references to the renamed class
- [ ] Verify sample still compiles and runs
- [ ] Document in code comments why the duplicate existed (for testing type ambiguity)

comments.author: Nathan Whittle

title: DOCS: Document manual beads export for bv fallback mode

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text: NOTE: This is a global beads workflow documentation issue, not specific to IoCTools v1.3.0. Not blocking release.

title: Develop AST-based script for safe assertion refactoring
description:
The regex/string replacement approach for adding severity assertions proved fragile and introduced bugs. Develop an AST-based Python script using Roslyn or a similar approach to safely add severity assertions to C# test files.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Script parses C# test files using AST (not regex)
- Correctly identifies diagnostic test patterns vs non-diagnostic patterns
- Safely adds severity assertions without breaking existing code
- Handles both variable-based and inline diagnostic assertion patterns

PARENT: ioc-5f1 (this would help complete that epic)

title: DECISION: Manual refactor vs automation investment for severity assertions
description:
Automation script for adding severity assertions failed once. Decide whether to invest in AST-based automation (ioc-atl) or do manual grunt work for the 101+ missing severity assertions.

OPTIONS:
1. Invest in AST-based automation - upfront cost, safer, reusable
2. Manual refactor - time-consuming but straightforward, no technical risk

DECISION NEEDED: Which approach should we take for ioc-5f1?

title: BUG: IOC081 Error severity prevents Sample project Release build
close_reason: Changed IOC081 severity from Error to Warning. Sample project now builds in Release mode. Tests updated to assert Warning severity.

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-6la

CONTEXT: IOC081 (Manual registration duplicates IoCTools registration) is at Error severity. The sample project intentionally demonstrates this diagnostic, but Error severity prevents the sample from compiling in Release mode.

OPTIONS:
1. Change IOC081 severity from Error to Warning
2. Restructure sample project to not use actual duplicate registrations

RECOMMENDATION: Change to Warning severity - duplicates are a code smell but not always a hard error. Users may intentionally have duplicates during migration.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Sample project compiles in Release mode
- [ ] IOC081 still reports (as Warning)
- [ ] Tests updated if needed

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DECISION NEEDED: Should IOC081 severity be Error or Warning?

OPTIONS:
1. Keep as Error - Duplicate registrations are a hard error, users must fix before build succeeds
   - Pro: Forces clean code, no surprises at runtime
   - Con: Breaks builds during migration, Sample project can't demonstrate the diagnostic

2. Change to Warning - Alert but don't fail the build
   - Pro: Better DX during migration, Sample can compile
   - Con: Users might ignore warnings, potential for duplicate registration issues at runtime

RECOMMENDATION: Warning seems more appropriate since duplicates are a code smell but not always wrong (migration scenarios, intentional overrides).

title: TASK: Update git remote URL to new location
description: Git push shows 'This repository moved. Please use the new location: https://github.com/sansiquay/IoCTools.git' on every push. Update the remote URL to avoid future breakage.

title: DOCS: Fix IoCToolsManualSeverity property name in Sample project and DiagnosticExamples

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-3hf

CONTEXT: While fixing MSBuildDiagnosticConfigurationTests, found that IoCTools.Sample/Program.cs:1597 and IoCTools.Sample/Services/DiagnosticExamples.cs:26 reference the non-existent property 'IoCToolsUnregisteredSeverity'. The actual property name is 'IoCToolsManualSeverity' (as seen in DiagnosticUtilities.cs).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Update Program.cs line 1597: IoCToolsUnregisteredSeverity → IoCToolsManualSeverity
- Update DiagnosticExamples.cs line 26: IoCToolsUnregisteredSeverity → IoCToolsManualSeverity
- Verify sample project builds and runs correctly

title: Audit and annotate non-diagnostic ContainSingle usages
description: Some ContainSingle() assertions are for collections (registration methods, generated sources, constructors), not diagnostics. These should be annotated to prevent false positives in global search/replace operations.

title: CI/CD Infrastructure Epic
description:
Establish comprehensive CI/CD infrastructure for IoCTools.

Current state:
- GitHub Actions workflow exists for NuGet publishing on version change
- No automated build verification before publish
- No automated test execution in CI
- Sample project build not validated in CI

Goals:
- Ensure all projects build successfully before publish
- Run full test suite (1200+ tests) on every PR and push
- Validate Sample project compiles (when IOC081 severity is fixed)
- Consider adding code coverage reporting
- Consider adding benchmark regression detection

title: Add build verification step to CI workflow
description: Add a build step that compiles all projects before NuGet publishing. Currently the workflow goes straight to publish without verifying the build succeeds.

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DoR missing: Acceptance Criteria, Verification plan. Needs discussion/spec before autonomous execution.

SUGGESTED ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All projects (Abstractions, Generator, Tests, Sample) build successfully
- Build step runs before publish step
- Build failure prevents publish from executing

SUGGESTED VERIFICATION:
- Trigger CI workflow and verify build step runs
- Intentionally break a build and verify publish is skipped

title: Add test execution to CI workflow
description: Run the full test suite (IoCTools.Generator.Tests) in CI on every PR and push to main. Tests should pass before any publish step executes.

- Test failure prevents publish from executing

- Intentionally fail a test and verify publish is skipped

title: Add Sample project build validation to CI
description: Ensure the Sample project compiles successfully in CI. This validates that the generated code works correctly in a real-world usage scenario. Note: Blocked until IOC081 severity is resolved (ioc-bn2).

title: Add PR build/test workflow
description: Create a separate GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull_request events to validate changes before merge. Should include build and test steps but NOT publish.

title: CLI: Output generated extension method source code
description:
Enhance the IoCTools CLI to output the generated source code for service collection extension methods.

**Current State:**
- services-path outputs path to generated registration extension file
- services outputs summary of registrations
- Users must navigate to file to see actual generated code

**Requested Feature:**
Add command (or flag) to output actual generated source code for:
1. Full extension method file (all registrations)
2. Registration code for specific service type (filtered view)

**Proposed Implementation:**
- New services-source command or --source flag on services
- Optional --type filter for specific service registrations
- Output to stdout for piping/redirection
close_reason: Implemented --source flag for services and fields commands with tests

title: FEATURE: Add MSBuild configuration for IOC08x diagnostic severities

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-bn2

CONTEXT: IOC001/002 have configurable MSBuild severity (IoCToolsNoImplementationSeverity, IoCToolsUnregisteredSeverity). IOC08x diagnostics (manual registration overlap) currently have fixed severity.

DECISION NEEDED: Should IOC082 (lifetime mismatch - Error) and IOC083 (options duplication - Error) be configurable?

OPTIONS:
1. Add configurability for all IOC08x - allows teams to enforce stricter or looser policies
2. Keep IOC082 as Error only (runtime bug risk) but allow IOC081/083 to be configured
3. Leave as-is - migration diagnostics should have fixed severity

RISK: IOC082 detects lifetime mismatches that cause runtime bugs. Allowing suppression could hide real issues.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (if implemented):
- [ ] New MSBuild properties (e.g., IoCToolsDuplicateRegistrationSeverity, IoCToolsLifetimeMismatchSeverity)
- [ ] Integration tests confirm properties override default severity
- [ ] Documentation updated

title: DOC: Document diagnostic severity specification in CLAUDE.md

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-2nl

CONTEXT: CLAUDE.md mentions diagnostics IOC001-IOC031 but doesn't provide a comprehensive reference of expected severities. Agents working on diagnostic tests need to look up DiagnosticDescriptors.cs to verify correct severity.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] CLAUDE.md has a table/list mapping diagnostic codes to their expected severity (Error/Warning/Info)
- [ ] Table includes at least IOC001-IOC086 (all current diagnostics)

title: v1.3.0 Release
description:
IoCTools v1.3.0 release preparation. This release includes significant analyzer improvements, new validators, and enhanced diagnostic capabilities.

Key themes:
- Implicit lifetime handling improvements
- Cross-assembly dependency awareness
- Hosted service lifecycle validation
- Framework dependency recognition
- Member name redundancy detection
- Enhanced options binding generation

title: DOCS: Add verification step to beads-reflect before creating bug beads

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-667 reflection

CONTEXT: ioc-667 was created claiming an 'ambiguous FeatureFlags reference' error, but when I went to fix it, no such error existed in the build output. The duplicate classes did exist (and were fixed), but the specific symptom was misdiagnosed or hallucinated during the reflection session that created the bead.

PROBLEM: Beads created from automated session reflections may include false positives if the reflection doesn't verify compilation errors actually exist before creating bug beads.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Update beads-reflect skill to require 'dotnet build' verification before creating BUG beads
- [ ] Add a 'verify the error exists' step to the discovery validation workflow
- [ ] Document the verification requirement in the skill

NOTE: This is a cross-project process improvement. May need to be moved to a global skills repo.
